primary_field: decision provenance and rationale (why X was chosen over Y, why a word was rejected, why a structure was adopted)

adjacents.field: regulatory path selection (Reg CF vs 506(c), Howey analysis, FTC exposure)
vendor selection (HelloSign vs DocuSign; Middesk vs Alloy; StartEngine vs Republic)
SEC-safe language decisions and trigger words

provenance: Instantiated B117 per #2276 Scribe Coverage Discovery — filling the decision_provenance category blind spot identified by K437 SEALED-50 run.

observation: Why the word 'Guaranteed' was rejected from the Cost-Slasher landing page callout (B116): (1) FTC deceptive-practices exposure — a guarantee without a refund rail is overclaim per 16 CFR 239.3; (2) weaker than the math — the evidence table already shows Haiku approx Opus accuracy at 1/19th cost, the adjective undercuts the evidence; (3) Founder-voice mismatch — Founder's rhetorical keystones understate, 'Guaranteed' reads marketing-suit not cooperative-operator.
canonical_ref: SCEV1 bank Q004 + AA_FORMAL_2272 Cost-Slasher section

observation: Why HelloSign was chosen over DocuSign in K432: lower per-envelope cost for startups; simpler API; free sandbox without credit card required. Per K432 Knight report B116.
canonical_ref: SCEV1 bank Q005 + K432 Knight report

observation: Why K438 (Member-facing Cathedral) waits on K437 results to dispatch: Prove it first. Product it second. canonical methodology, Founder-ratified B116. Building a member feature before empirical validation would invert proof and commitment. If SCEV-1 shows weak lift, K438 architecture gets adjusted before ship. Same discipline as Chapter 3 landing-page teaser holding until empirical numbers arrive.

Why Herjavec RPA splits at exactly 50% of Founder's 20% slice (NOT 70/30 or 80/20 or off-the-top): (1) nothing to do with anyone else — Founder's 20% is his own to dispose commercially, affects zero cooperative members; (2) belief is signaled by symmetry — half is the only number that means what it says, removes 'why not 50/50' negotiation; (3) Founder's explicit goal is bounded wealth + escape velocity, not maximum extraction; (4) small piece of huge pie beats all of small pie — escape velocity funds everything next. Out-of-founder-half structure, not off-the-top, preserves 60/20/10/10 member-facing math with no asterisk.
canonical_ref: SCEV1 bank Q025 + AA_FORMAL_2239 Herjavec section + project_herjavec_deal_structure_b097.md

observation: Why .gitignore *.json is a reproducibility bomb for librarian-mcp: rule matches librarian-mcp/package.json and librarian-mcp/tsconfig.json, making them untracked. K436 Knight observed during commit. A fresh clone of LianaBanyanPlatform cannot build librarian-mcp — dependencies and TS config irrecoverable without local-disk access. K441 Half B fix: explicit ! negations in .gitignore for specific runtime-critical files + git add -f to stage.
canonical_ref: SCEV1 bank Q026 + PROMPT_KNIGHT_K441 Half B

observation: Why Upekrithen LLC acts as seller-of-record for Pedestal Stakes (NOT Liana Banyan Corp directly): (1) arms-length transaction layer — separate legal person avoids self-dealing appearance in cooperative governance; (2) legitimate proceeds flow via inter-company loan / additional paid-in capital / expense reimbursement; (3) bankruptcy/liability isolation — challenges run against asset-holding LLC not operating cooperative; (4) QSBS preservation (LLC-formed-before-Corp); (5) matches Claim D2 of IP Load Balancing Scaffold — founder-adjacent economic activity flows through neutral holding entity.
canonical_ref: SCEV1 bank Q027 + AA_FORMAL_2239 Upekrithen section

observation: Why 'under-promise, over-deliver' is positioned as a sub-principle of 'Prove it first. Product it second.': under-promise-over-deliver governs public-timing commitments specifically. Prove-Then-Product is the cross-layer law (marketing, product, economic). Public dates are a commitment layer where the discipline cashes out as: set the date conservatively so that delivery is the proof. Example B116: Chapter 2 Mellon teaser updated from 'coming this weekend' to 'coming next week' — same evening Chapter 2 went live, validating the discipline on its first test case.

Why Glass Door protocol (#2262, Crown Jewel) is 'public-by-default outreach with member-voted dispatch': transparency-first — letters drafted in public, reviewed by members, voted on, dispatched only after community sign-off. Rationale: a cooperative platform cannot run private PR campaigns in its members' names; accountability runs both ways. K412 deployed system B113; 95 letters loaded into queue.

Why Reg CF was chosen over Reg D 506(c) for Pedestal Stake offering (B114 counsel ruling): 'yes on my read' — Reg CF as primary path. Parallel 506(c) architecture compatibility preserved for later activation; not pursued at launch.
canonical_ref: SCEV1 bank Q020 + B114 closeout counsel ruling

observation: Bill Gates is on INDEFINITE HOLD for crown letters due to Epstein association. Melinda French Gates is NOT on hold — proceed as normal target. MEMORY.md line 59 corrected B117. Prior 'Gates ON HOLD (Epstein)' was ambiguous and conflated the two; Founder clarified in B117 exchange.
canonical_ref: MEMORY.md B117 edit + Founder B117 correction

observation: K437 SCEV-1 preliminary PASS threshold decision: >=5pp mean accuracy lift cathedral-over-base. K437 SEALED-50 cleared at +6.0pp lenient / +5.0pp strict — PASSES both grading conventions. Cleared criterion but margin much tighter than SEED-18's +19.4pp. Interpretation: SEED-18 was Bishop-curated with Cathedral-awareness (favorable bias); SEALED-50 includes 3 blind-spot categories producing 0pp. Where Cathedral has content, lift is real (+11-17pp).

K438 Member Cathedral sequencing decision (B117 Founder): Option B chosen — expand Scribes for 3 blind-spot categories (architecture_continuity, decision_provenance, founder_voice), re-run K437 SEALED-50, ship K438 on stronger numbers. Rationale: +6.0pp pooled is honest but weak member pitch. +15-20pp expected after expansion justifies ~1 session delay. Prove-Then-Product says ship stronger evidence, not weaker.

Why the AI Companion (#2275) is positioned as 'infrastructure' NOT 'consumer product': the Companion is the vendor-neutral bridge that keeps member memory with the user not with the AI vendor. When AI majors (Anthropic/OpenAI/Google) ship native memory features, Companion becomes the reason members don't get trapped. Framing: 'Keep your AI memory with you, not with whichever AI company you use today.' NOT 'A better ChatGPT Memory.'

Why 5 trademarks for $1,250 (NotCents + The Baton + Librarian MCP + Liana Banyan + 1 TBD): 5 x $250 USPTO TEAS Plus single-class. Founder pushed back in B064 exchange 'that's still a lot of money at $250 per' — Bishop narrowed to 'at least these two' (NotCents + The Baton per B103 canon). Sweep B117 recovered 4-of-5 names; 5th still TBD.

Why Mercury (mercury.com) chosen as LB Corporation's primary business bank: startup-oriented, API-friendly, no-minimum-balance, integrates with Stripe/QuickBooks. `MecuryIsRising` env var in DOUBLESECRET.env is the Mercury API key (rename target: MERCURY_API_KEY for canonical consistency). Distinct from LB Card product (Netspend/Green Dot/Stripe Issuing) — Mercury is the operating account, LB Card is the card offering.

decision: Canonical sync strategy B118: on discovering canonical_values.yaml staleness during librarian rebuild, commit YAML immediately as standalone chore, not batch with other work. Pre-commit hooks passed clean (gitleaks, YAML validation). Commit 02e52b7 caught up K421+Prov13+B117 backlog in one diff (innovation_count 2250→2270, crown_jewels 216→228, formal_claims_approximate 2405→2506, +touchstone_r10 block). Pattern: single-source-of-truth files deserve immediate commit on edit, regardless of whether related work is mid-flight.

Prov 14 B118 posture: KEEP OPEN. Founder explicit: 'let another accumulate'. Rationale: leave-open strategy has already paid off twice (#2276 + #2277 ratified B117 after prior filing window). Costs: every open day is a day Cathedral architecture is undisclosed-but-public-ish via PyPI + landing page. Benefit: ≥1 additional CJ likely to materialize across the 12-hour B118 remainder. Decision defaults to the benefit side until a new CJ lands or Founder flips the trigger.

K447 CI approach: standalone .github/workflows/supabase-pgtap.yml, separate from existing cephas-firebase-deploy.yml. Scope: pgTAP assertion-only gate (reporting), not deploy-on-green (K448+). Deploy-gate comments inserted into cathedral-export + cathedral-import edge-function entry files as reviewer-visible reminders — not runtime checks. First CI run is live-validation (no local Docker during authoring); if red, fix forward as K447a.
Three Fates enforcement requires BOTH cultural preamble strengthening AND structural daemon (SP-24), not either alone
why_both: Cultural-only fails — Bishop B119 demonstrated this in the living example by running Fates selectively despite feedback_three_fates_scribe_routing.md being in preamble. Structural-only is brittle because new agent types / Chessboard members won't know WHY the daemon is catching their output. Both together: cultural explains the purpose, structural enforces the behavior.
who_affected: All Chessboard agents (Bishop/Knight/Rook/Pawn) + future agent types
next_action: Write SP-24 Three Fates Daemon Knight prompt

decision: Trust Match probation refund curve = 100% for first 1/3 of window, linear decay to 0% at window end
why: Linear decay is simpler UX than exponential or cliff curves. 1/3 full-refund window gives immediate recourse with clear urgency; cliff-refund alone creates gaming incentive to time the cliff. Manual refund request always available during probation as escape hatch. Applies uniformly across all stake sizes and project-counts.
